items.title: Services Are the New Software
items.summary: Sequoia is reframing AI's TAM from the ~$650B software market to the ~$10T services economy: agents that deliver outcomes (legal, accounting, support, wealth) rather than seats. Implication: pricing shifts from per-seat SaaS to per-outcome/per-action, defensibility lives in workflow + proprietary data, and incumbents whose moat is human labor margins are now in the strike zone.

items.title: Open-Source AI as Geopolitical Stack
items.summary: Horowitz argues the US is losing the open-source AI race to China (DeepSeek lineage), and that whoever controls the default open weights controls the encoded values of billions of devices. Capital implication: a16z is positioning portfolio bets around US open-weight labs, sovereign compute, and the policy fight to legalize broad open release. DeepSeek's permanent 75% V4-Pro price cut this week is the live ammunition for that thesis.

items.title: Foothills-of-Singularity Compute Buildout
items.summary: Sequoia ("2026: This is AGI") and Hassabis at I/O are aligning the narrative that agentic capability is now real and the binding constraint is compute, memory, and power. Capital is moving into HBM, advanced packaging, nuclear/grid, and orchestration layers ("harnesses") rather than another foundation-model bet. With memory now ~2/3 of AI chip BOM (Epoch), the alpha is in the picks-and-shovels around the frontier model layer.

items.title: AI-Native Visibility & Distribution
items.summary: Peec hitting $10M ARR in months tracking brand presence inside AI answers signals a new category: SEO is being replaced by 'AI visibility' / answer-engine optimization. Money will follow tools that let brands measure, influence, and arbitrage how LLMs surface them - the same playbook that funded the SEO/martech stack a decade ago, on a compressed timeline.

items.title: Vertical AI Eating Regulated Services
items.summary: Sequoia's Nevis (wealth management) and Ineffable Intelligence (superlearner) bets, paired with their '$1T Next Amazon in Retail' essay, show capital concentrating on vertical AI plays inside large, regulated, advisor-heavy markets. The wedge: AI-native workflow + compliance + proprietary data, sold to incumbents as a services-replacement rather than a tool.

items.title: Google ships Gemini 3.5 + Omni and 'Antigravity 2.0' agent harness at I/O
items.summary: I/O 2026 closed with Hassabis declaring the 'foothills of the singularity' alongside two new Gemini models and demos of Antigravity 2.0 autonomously building an OS for under $1,000. Strategically, Google is binding its agent layer into Search, Maps (Genie world model), and Workspace, raising the cost of switching for both consumers and enterprises and squeezing standalone agent startups that don't own distribution.

items.title: Anthropic's Code with Claude pushes 'fully AI-written PRs' as table stakes
items.summary: Anthropic's London dev event normalized the idea that shipping pull requests entirely written by Claude is the new baseline for software teams - echoing Brockman's claim that ~80% of OpenAI's code is AI-written. The strategic shift: dev tooling vendors now compete on agent orchestration, sandboxing, and review surfaces, not autocomplete.

items.title: DeepSeek makes 75% V4-Pro price cut permanent
items.summary: DeepSeek locked in V4-Pro pricing at a quarter of launch rates - frontier reasoning at roughly an order of magnitude below US labs, runnable on Huawei Ascend. The strategic implication: a permanent floor on inference pricing globally, pressure on OpenAI/Anthropic/Google gross margins, and a tailwind for any startup whose unit economics were blocked by API costs.

items.title: Stitch 3.0 by Google (Product Hunt)
items.summary: Validates Foothills-of-Singularity Compute Buildout: Stitch 3.0 is Google's AI design-to-code product, the consumer/dev-facing surface of the same Gemini stack Hassabis used to frame the 'foothills' narrative. It's the wedge that turns the agent harness into shipped UI and a direct competitor to design-first AI tools - showing how the frontier lab pushes its capability glut downstream into product workflows.

items.title: Runway Agent (Product Hunt)
items.summary: Validates Services Are the New Software: Runway Agent moves the company from creative tool to autonomous production agent - the user describes an outcome (a finished video/asset) and the agent executes the multi-step pipeline. This is the services-as-software pattern applied to media/marketing services, the exact playbook Sequoia is funding across verticals.

items.title: ModelHub (Product Hunt)
items.summary: Validates Open-Source AI as Geopolitical Stack: ModelHub is an aggregation/routing layer across open-weight and proprietary models - exactly the infrastructure that makes DeepSeek's permanent price cut consumable for Western developers without re-platforming. As open weights commoditize, value migrates to routing, eval, and governance layers like this.

items.title: Answer-Engine Optimization tooling for the Fortune 5000
items.summary: Peec's $10M ARR proves brands will pay now to track and influence their presence in ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity answers. Who can capture: martech operators with existing CMO relationships who bolt on AEO measurement + content rewrite agents. What must be true: LLMs remain a material share of branded queries and citation behavior stays measurable. Window: next two quarters before HubSpot/Semrush ship native.

items.title: Migrate cost-sensitive agent workloads to DeepSeek V4-Pro this quarter
items.summary: With V4-Pro permanently at ~25% of launch price and cache-hit pricing near zero, any startup whose burn is dominated by inference can re-tier the stack: DeepSeek for bulk reasoning, US frontier models for sensitive/regulated workloads. Who captures: pre-Series-B agent and codegen companies whose gross margin was -20% to +20%. What must be true: enterprise buyers tolerate a Chinese model in non-sensitive paths (often yes via routing). Window: 60-90 days before competitors do the same.

items.title: Edgee Fallback Models (Product Hunt)
items.summary: Validates Open-Source AI as Geopolitical Stack: Edgee's fallback-model routing is the operational wedge for the DeepSeek arbitrage above - it lets a team plug in cheap open-weight models with automatic failover to a Western lab, which is the only way enterprises will actually adopt the price cut. For operators, this is a buy-not-build today.

items.title: AI-native wealth and advisory wrappers
items.summary: Sequoia backing Nevis signals that the wealth-management book of business is now an acquisition target for AI-native challengers. Who captures: ex-Fintech/advisor founders who can pair a compliance-ready agent with a CFP-style human-in-the-loop. What must be true: regulators tolerate AI-led advice with disclosed oversight (already happening in pilots). Window: this fiscal year, before incumbents (Schwab, Fidelity) ship comparable.

items.title: Outcome-priced agent platforms displacing per-seat SaaS
items.summary: If Sequoia is right that AI eats services, the pricing model has to change from $/seat to $/ticket-resolved, $/return-filed, $/case-closed. Who captures: vertical AI companies that own the system of record AND the billing relationship (legal, accounting, support, claims). What must be true: enterprises accept variable spend on AI outcomes, and SLAs become enforceable. 12-18 month horizon as procurement catches up.

items.title: Memory-centric AI infra (HBM, CXL, near-memory compute)
items.summary: Memory now sits at ~two-thirds of AI chip BOM. Over 6-18 months that re-orders the infra opportunity stack: HBM suppliers, CXL fabric startups, KV-cache/eviction software, and memory-aware schedulers become the bottleneck winners. Who captures: deep-tech infra teams and chip-adjacent software. What must be true: agentic workloads continue to push context windows and persistent memory.

items.title: Long-horizon proactive personal assistants
items.summary: π-Bench formalizes evaluation for proactive long-horizon agents - the missing benchmark for the 'personal CEO/agent' product category Brockman keeps describing. Over 6-18 months whoever leads this benchmark and ships a credible consumer-grade proactive agent (calendar, comms, finances) can re-anchor the smartphone/wearable UX. Who captures: consumer-AI teams with distribution (Apple, Amazon Bee, OpenAI) or unique data moats.

items.title: AI wearables as the next ambient interface
items.summary: Amazon's Bee plus Xreal's smart-glasses progress with Google point to a 6-18 month window where wearable + on-device LLM crosses the usability line. Who captures: Apple, Google, Meta, plus a small set of audio/AR startups with privacy-credible memory products. What must be true: regulators don't kill always-on recording, and battery/latency improvements ship. The privacy backlash is the real swing variable.

items.title: AI-led scientific discovery as a category, not a feature
items.summary: Hassabis's WeatherNext and Isomorphic Labs framing - AI not as a productivity tool for scientists but as a scientist - points to an 18+ month directional bet on AI-native research orgs (drug discovery, materials, climate). Who captures: vertically integrated labs that own model + wet lab + data, not pure software vendors. What must be true: at least one AI-discovered drug or material clears a real-world bar by 2028.

items.title: World models as the substrate for embodied AI
items.summary: PhysX-Omni's unified simulation-ready 3D generation, plus Google using its Genie world model inside Maps, signals that world models are graduating from research curiosity to deployable infra for robotics and AR. Who captures: robotics foundation-model startups + sim-to-real platforms. What must be true: world models become controllable and physically accurate enough for closed-loop training. 18-36 month window.

items.title: Sovereign / open-weight AI stacks as national infrastructure
items.summary: If Horowitz's framing wins, governments will fund national open-weight models the way they fund GPS or highways. Who captures: US open-weight labs (and their compute partners), EU sovereign-AI plays, plus the policy/compliance tooling layer. What must be true: a US administration explicitly backs open release as strategic, and DeepSeek-class models keep closing the gap. 18+ month bet with binary policy risk.

items.title: Compute-as-collateral and energy-linked AI financing
items.summary: Deep Fission's IPO attempt and xAI/SpaceX's orbital-data-center bets foreshadow a financial layer where GPU contracts, PPAs, and even reactors get securitized to fund AI capex. Who captures: project-finance shops, hyperscaler treasuries, and a new wave of AI-energy SPVs. What must be true: AI compute demand stays above hurdle rates for 5-10 year energy assets. Paradigm shift in how the AI stack is financed.

leader_voices.name: Demis Hassabis
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Closing Google I/O 2026, Hassabis said attendees were 'standing in the foothills of the singularity,' framing the new Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 launches plus Antigravity 2.0 agent demos as the opening stage of a deeper transformation, with AGI possible within five years and impact he described as 10x the Industrial Revolution at 10x the speed.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: Expect Google to keep binding agents into Search, Maps and Workspace; operators should assume agentic features become table stakes in 12-18 months and price product roadmaps accordingly.

leader_voices.name: Yann LeCun
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: In the same news cycle as Hassabis's 'foothills' speech, LeCun publicly maintained that current AI systems are not genuinely intelligent, pushing back on imminent-AGI framing from Google and OpenAI.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: The widening leader split on AGI timing is itself the signal: investors should discount AGI-marketing premia and underwrite companies on near-term unit economics, not narrative.

leader_voices.name: Greg Brockman
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: On The Knowledge Project (re-circulating heavily this week) Brockman said it is now 'hard to know what percent' of OpenAI's code is not written by AI, and described compute as 'a revenue centre, not a cost centre,' with OpenAI committing essentially all available capital to it.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: Coding-agent capability is being normalized at the top lab; dev-tools companies need to reposition above the autocomplete layer, and any infra play that monetizes inference is now in a structurally favored category.

leader_voices.name: Ben Horowitz
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: In a16z's re-released conversation on open-source AI, Horowitz argued that Chinese developers have already 'captured the open-source heartbeat of global AI through DeepSeek,' and warned that closed-only US policy hands cultural and technical defaults to Beijing's model weights.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: a16z is publicly tying its AI portfolio to an open-weight, pro-American-release policy stance; expect more LP-visible capital flowing to US open-weight labs and the routing/compliance layer that lets enterprises consume them safely.

leader_voices.name: Oriol Vinyals
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Vinyals split the difference on the AGI debate this week, saying today's models would have looked like AGI seven years ago but still lack the ability to learn from experience or produce real breakthroughs.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: Even insiders concede current models are bounded by experiential learning - a clear signal for investors to back continual-learning, memory and RL-from-experience plays rather than yet another base model.

leader_voices.name: Clément Delangue
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: On the MTS podcast (May 22), Delangue argued the real bubble is in API-based large language models, not open weights, and that robotics is likely the next major interface for AI.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: If proprietary-API margins compress while open-weight + robotics expand, value migrates to hardware, robotics foundation models and inference infra - reinforcing this week's DeepSeek and memory-cost data points.

leader_voices.name: Chi Xu
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Google's smart-glasses partner Xreal's CEO told TechCrunch he believes the smart-glasses business has finally reached a turning point, with the product category ready to scale alongside Gemini-powered features.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: AI wearables are exiting the trough - operators should assume a real consumer market in 12-18 months and stake distribution, content and privacy-tooling positions now.

top_signals.headline: DeepSeek locks in 75% V4-Pro price cut permanently, resetting global inference pricing
top_signals.why_it_matters: Frontier reasoning is now permanently at ~25% of launch cost on Huawei Ascend, compressing US lab gross margins and unblocking unit economics for any agent/codegen startup that re-tiers its stack this quarter.

top_signals.headline: Hassabis declares 'foothills of the singularity' at Google I/O as Gemini 3.5/Omni + Antigravity 2.0 ship
top_signals.why_it_matters: Google is binding agents into Search, Maps and Workspace at the same moment it makes the loudest AGI claim of the year - standalone agent startups without distribution face an immediate moat problem.

top_signals.headline: Memory is now ~two-thirds of AI chip BOM
top_signals.why_it_matters: Capital allocators should rotate part of the 'AI infra' bucket from compute-only to memory (HBM, CXL, KV-cache software) - it's the binding constraint for the next 18 months of agentic workloads.

top_signals.headline: Sequoia's 'Services are the New Software' becomes the dominant VC frame
top_signals.why_it_matters: Pricing, GTM and defensibility frameworks for AI companies are being rewritten around outcomes, not seats; founders pitching per-seat SaaS into 2026 boards will see valuation pressure.

top_signals.headline: Peec hits $10M ARR tracking brand presence inside AI answers
top_signals.why_it_matters: Answer-engine optimization is now a real, fundable category with paying enterprise logos - a wedge window of 1-2 quarters before HubSpot/Semrush ship native AEO.

weekly_theme: Foothills of the singularity meet a permanent price war: capital pivots from frontier models to the agents, memory and open-weight infrastructure underneath them.
theme_context: This week Google I/O's 'foothills of the singularity' framing collided with DeepSeek's decision to make a 75% V4-Pro price cut permanent, while Sequoia's AI Ascent essays declared services - not software - as the real $10T AI TAM. The combined message to operators and investors is that the frontier-model layer is commoditizing on price even as agentic capability accelerates, and the alpha is shifting to the infrastructure (memory, routing, compute financing), the distribution layer (AEO, wearables, vertical SaaS-replacement), and the policy fight over who controls open weights.
